What is the percentage of superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code? Indicate how many of the said superheroes were published by Marvel Comics.

Hint: published by Marvel Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'; superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code refers to alignment = 'Bad'; calculation = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(alignment = 'Bad); count(id)), 100)

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-3#opus48-r1", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage, SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marvel_bad_count FROM superhero s LEFT JOIN alignment a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id;", "base_task_id": "bird-train-3"}

Among all the atoms from 21 to 25, list all the molecules that are carcinogenic.

Hint: atoms from 21 to 25 refers to SUBSTR(atom_id, 7, 2) between '21' and '25'; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-15#opus48-r1", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT DISTINCT T2.molecule_id FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE SUBSTR(T1.atom_id, 7, 2) BETWEEN '21' AND '25' AND T2.label = '+'", "base_task_id": "bird-train-15"}

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `element` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `bond_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`,`atom_id2`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id2`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`bond_id`) REFERENCES `bond` (`bond_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
);
CREATE TABLE `molecule` (
  `molecule_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `label` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`molecule_id`)
);
{"command": "sqlite3 database.db \"SELECT atom_id, SUBSTR(atom_id,7,2) FROM atom LIMIT 5;\""}

Calculate the total atoms with triple-bond molecules containing the element phosphorus or bromine.

Hint: triple bond refers to bond_type = '#'; phosphorus refers to element = 'p'; bromine refers to element = 'br'

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-14#opus46-r1", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1", "split": "train", "reward": 0.0, "final_answer": "SELECT COUNT(T1.atom_id) FROM atom T1 WHERE T1.molecule_id IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.molecule_id FROM bond T2 INNER JOIN atom T3 ON T2.molecule_id = T3.molecule_id WHERE T2.bond_type = '#' AND (T3.element = 'p' OR T3.element = 'br'))", "base_task_id": "bird-train-14"}

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `element` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `bond_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`,`atom_id2`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id2`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`bond_id`) REFERENCES `bond` (`bond_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
);
CREATE TABLE `molecule` (
  `molecule_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `label` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`molecule_id`)
);

Consider the average difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment of schools that are locally funded, list the names and DOC type of schools which has a difference above this average.

Hint: Difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment can be computed by `Enrollment (K-12)` - `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-41#opus46-r1", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1", "split": "train", "reward": 0.0, "final_answer": "SELECT s.School, s.DOCType FROM schools s JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode WHERE s.FundingType = 'Locally funded' AND (f.`Enrollment (K-12)` - f.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) > (SELECT AVG(f2.`Enrollment (K-12)` - f2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm f2 JOIN schools s2 ON f2.CDSCode = s2.CDSCode WHERE s2.FundingType = 'Locally funded')", "base_task_id": "bird-train-41"}

CREATE TABLE frpm
(
    CDSCode                                       TEXT not null
        primary key,
    `Academic Year`                               TEXT  null,
    `County Code`                                 TEXT  null,
    `District Code`                               INTEGER         null,
    `School Code`                                 TEXT  null,
    `County Name`                                 TEXT null,
    `District Name`                               TEXT null,
    `School Name`                                 TEXT null,
    `District Type`                               TEXT null,
    `School Type`                                 TEXT null,
    `Educational Option Type`                     TEXT null,
    `NSLP Provision Status`                       TEXT null,
    `Charter School (Y/N)`                        INTEGER    null,
    `Charter School Number`                       TEXT  null,
    `Charter Funding Type`                        TEXT null,
    IRC                                           INTEGER    null,
    `Low Grade`                                   TEXT  null,
    `High Grade`                                  TEXT null,

Calculate the total atoms with triple-bond molecules containing the element phosphorus or bromine.

Hint: triple bond refers to bond_type = '#'; phosphorus refers to element = 'p'; bromine refers to element = 'br'

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-14#opus48-r2", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT COUNT(T.atom_id) FROM atom AS T INNER JOIN bond AS B ON T.molecule_id = B.molecule_id WHERE B.bond_type = '#' AND (T.element = 'p' OR T.element = 'br');", "base_task_id": "bird-train-14"}

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,

  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `bond_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`,`atom_id2`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id2`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`bond_id`) REFERENCES `bond` (`bond_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
);
CREATE TABLE `molecule` (
  `molecule_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `label` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`molecule_id`)
);

Calculate the average number of oxygen atoms in single-bonded molecules.

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `element` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `bond_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`,`atom_id2`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id2`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`bond_id`) REFERENCES `bond` (`bond_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE

{"command": "sqlite3 database.db \"SELECT AVG(oxygen_count) FROM (SELECT b.molecule_id, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.element = 'o' THEN 1 END) AS oxygen_count FROM bond b INNER JOIN atom a ON b.molecule_id = a.molecule_id WHERE b.bond_type = '-' GROUP BY b.molecule_id);\""}

Was each expense in October Meeting on October 8, 2019 approved?

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-38#opus48-r2", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8", "split": "train", "reward": 0.0, "final_answer": "SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = SUM(CASE WHEN e.approved = 'true' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) THEN 'Yes, each expense was approved' ELSE 'No, not every expense was approved' END AS all_approved FROM expense e JOIN budget b ON e.link_to_budget = b.budget_id JOIN event ev ON b.link_to_event = ev.event_id WHERE ev.event_name = 'October Meeting' AND ev.event_date LIKE '2019-10-08%';", "base_task_id": "bird-train-38"}

What is the percentage of superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code? Indicate how many of the said superheroes were published by Marvel Comics.

Hint: published by Marvel Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'; superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code refers to alignment = 'Bad'; calculation = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(alignment = 'Bad); count(id)), 100)

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-3#opus47-r2", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage, SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marvel_bad_count FROM superhero s LEFT JOIN alignment a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id;", "base_task_id": "bird-train-3"}

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `element` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `bond_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`,`atom_id2`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id2`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`bond_id`) REFERENCES `bond` (`bond_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
);
CREATE TABLE `molecule` (
  `molecule_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `label` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`molecule_id`)
);

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-15#opus47-r2", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT DISTINCT T1.molecule_id FROM molecule AS T1 INNER JOIN atom AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE SUBSTR(T2.atom_id, 7, 2) BETWEEN '21' AND '25' AND T1.label = '+';", "base_task_id": "bird-train-15"}

What is the percentage of superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code? Indicate how many of the said superheroes were published by Marvel Comics.

Hint: published by Marvel Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'; superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code refers to alignment = 'Bad'; calculation = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(alignment = 'Bad); count(id)), 100)

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-3#opus47-r3", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage, SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marvel_bad_count FROM superhero s LEFT JOIN alignment a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id;", "base_task_id": "bird-train-3"}

Calculate the total average cost that Elijah Allen spent in the events on September and October.

CREATE TABLE alignment
(
    id        INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    alignment TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE attribute
(
    id             INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    attribute_name TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE colour
(
    id     INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    colour TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE gender
(
    id     INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    gender TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE publisher
(
    id             INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    publisher_name TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE race
(
    id   INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    race TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE superhero
(
    id             INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    superhero_name TEXT default NULL,
    full_name      TEXT default NULL,
    gender_id      INTEGER          default NULL,
    eye_colour_id  INTEGER          default NULL,
    hair_colour_id INTEGER          default NULL,
    skin_colour_id INTEGER          default NULL,
    race_id        INTEGER          default NULL,
    publisher_id   INTEGER          default NULL,
    alignment_id   INTEGER          default NULL,
    height_cm      INTEGER          default NULL,
    weight_kg      INTEGER          default NULL,
    foreign key (alignment_id) references alignment(id),
    foreign key (eye_colour_id) references colour(id),
    foreign key (gender_id) references gender(id),
    foreign key (hair_colour_id) references colour(id),
    foreign key (publisher_id) references publisher(id),
    foreign key (race_id) references race(id),
    foreign key (skin_colour_id) references colour(id)
);
CREATE TABLE hero_attribute
(
    hero_id         INTEGER default NULL,
    attribute_id    INTEGER default NULL,
    attribute_value INTEGER default NULL,
    foreign key (attribute_id) references attribute(id),
    foreign key (hero_id) references superhero(id)
);
CREATE TABLE superpower
(
    id         INTEGER not null
            primary key,
    power_name TEXT default NULL
);
CREATE TABLE hero_power
(
    hero_id  INTEGER default NULL,
    power_id INTEGER default NULL,
    foreign key (hero_id) references superhero(id),
    foreign key (power_id) references superpower(id)
);

Calculate the total atoms with triple-bond molecules containing the element phosphorus or bromine.

Hint: triple bond refers to bond_type = '#'; phosphorus refers to element = 'p'; bromine refers to element = 'br'

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-14#opus48-r4", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM atom WHERE (element = 'p' OR element = 'br') AND molecule_id IN (SELECT molecule_id FROM bond WHERE bond_type = '#');", "base_task_id": "bird-train-14"}

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `element` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,

What is the percentage of superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code? Indicate how many of the said superheroes were published by Marvel Comics.

Hint: published by Marvel Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'; superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code refers to alignment = 'Bad'; calculation = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(alignment = 'Bad); count(id)), 100)

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-3#opus47-r4", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage, SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marvel_bad_count FROM superhero s LEFT JOIN alignment a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id;", "base_task_id": "bird-train-3"}

CREATE TABLE `atom` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,

  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `bond` (
  `bond_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `molecule_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  `bond_type` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`bond_id`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`molecule_id`) REFERENCES `molecule` (`molecule_id`)
);
CREATE TABLE `connected` (
  `atom_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `atom_id2` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `bond_id` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`atom_id`,`atom_id2`),
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  FOREIGN KEY (`atom_id2`) REFERENCES `atom` (`atom_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,

);

  `molecule_id` TEXT NOT NULL,
  `label` TEXT DEFAULT NULL,

);

CREATE TABLE "attendance"
(
    link_to_event  TEXT,
    link_to_member TEXT,
    primary key (link_to_event, link_to_member),
    foreign key (link_to_event) references event(event_id),
    foreign key (link_to_member) references member(member_id)
);
CREATE TABLE "budget"
(
    budget_id     TEXT
            primary key,

    spent         REAL,

    link_to_event TEXT,
    foreign key (link_to_event) references event(event_id)
);
CREATE TABLE "expense"
(

    foreign key (link_to_budget) references budget(budget_id),
    foreign key (link_to_member) references member(member_id)
);
CREATE TABLE "income"
(
    income_id      TEXT
        constraint income_pk
            primary key,
    date_received  TEXT,
    amount         INTEGER,
    source         TEXT,
    notes          TEXT,
    link_to_member TEXT,
    foreign key (link_to_member) references member(member_id)
);
CREATE TABLE "member"
(
    member_id     TEXT
        constraint member_pk
            primary key,
    first_name    TEXT,
    last_name     TEXT,
    email         TEXT,
    position      TEXT,
    t_shirt_size  TEXT,
    phone         TEXT,
    zip           INTEGER,
    link_to_major TEXT,
    foreign key (link_to_major) references major(major_id),
    foreign key (zip) references zip_code(zip_code)
);

List the names of virtual schools that are among the top 5 in their respective counties based on average reading scores.

Hint: Exclusively virtual refers to Virtual = 'F'; respective counties means PARTITION BY County

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-42#opus48-r5", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-8", "split": "train", "reward": 0.0, "final_answer": "SELECT School FROM (
  SELECT sc.School AS School,
         RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY sc.County ORDER BY ss.AvgScrRead DESC) AS rnk
  FROM satscores ss
  JOIN schools sc ON ss.cds = sc.CDSCode
  WHERE sc.Virtual = 'F' AND ss.AvgScrRead IS NOT NULL
) WHERE rnk <= 5;", "base_task_id": "bird-train-42"}

What is the percentage of superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code? Indicate how many of the said superheroes were published by Marvel Comics.

Hint: published by Marvel Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'; superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code refers to alignment = 'Bad'; calculation = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(alignment = 'Bad); count(id)), 100)

{"benchmark": "bird-sql", "task_id": "bird-train-3#opus47-r5", "model": "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7", "split": "train", "reward": 1.0, "final_answer": "SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage, SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marvel_bad_count FROM superhero s LEFT JOIN alignment a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id;", "base_task_id": "bird-train-3"}
